all.en: My favorite baseball team reached the championship final after a long, difficult season.
all.zh: 我最喜愛的棒球隊在漫長而艱辛的賽季後闖進了總冠軍賽。

all.en: My father surprised me with two tickets to the final game as a birthday present.
all.zh: 父親給了我一份生日驚喜，那就是兩張總冠軍賽的門票。

all.en: The stadium was completely packed with over forty thousand cheering fans.
all.zh: 球場裡擠滿了超過四萬名歡呼的球迷。

all.en: Everyone was wearing team jerseys, waving flags and singing traditional cheering songs.
all.zh: 每個人都穿著球隊球衣、揮舞旗幟並唱著傳統的應援歌曲。

all.en: The atmosphere felt electric, and I could feel the excitement vibrating through my whole body.
all.zh: 現場氣氛電力十足，我能感受到那份興奮在我全身震動。

all.en: In the first inning, our pitcher struck out three batters in a row.
all.zh: 在第一局，我們的投手連續三振了三名打者。

all.en: The crowd roared so loudly that I could not hear my father's voice beside me.
all.zh: 觀眾的吼聲如此震耳欲聾，連在我身旁的父親說話聲都聽不見。

all.en: In the middle of the game, we shared hot dogs, popcorn and ice-cold drinks.
all.zh: 比賽中段，我們分享著熱狗、爆米花和冰涼的飲料。

all.en: By the ninth inning, the score remained tied, and tension filled every corner of the stadium.
all.zh: 到了第九局，比分仍然平手，緊張的氣氛瀰漫到球場的每個角落。

all.en: With two outs, our cleanup hitter sent the ball flying over the outfield wall.
all.zh: 在兩出局的情況下，我們的第四棒打者把球轟出了外野牆。

all.en: Strangers around us hugged each other and cried tears of pure happiness.
all.zh: 我們周圍的陌生人互相擁抱，流下了純粹喜悅的淚水。

all.en: Watching that championship game live with my father is a memory I will always treasure.
all.zh: 和父親一起現場觀看那場總冠軍賽，是一段我將永遠珍藏的回憶。

all.en: After the final out, my father and I joined the crowd singing the victory anthem.
all.zh: 最後一個出局之後，我和父親加入人群一同唱起勝利之歌。

all.en: We waited nearly an hour to leave the stadium because the exits were packed.
all.zh: 我們等了將近一小時才離開球場，因為出口擠滿了人。

all.en: Outside, fans were dancing in the streets and honking car horns in celebration.
all.zh: 場外球迷在街上跳舞，並按著喇叭歡慶勝利。

all.en: My father bought me a championship cap and a poster signed by the team.
all.zh: 父親幫我買了一頂冠軍紀念帽和一張球隊簽名的海報。

all.en: On the train home, we replayed every key moment of the game in detail.
all.zh: 在回家的火車上，我們把比賽的每個關鍵時刻都仔細回味了一遍。

all.en: My mother greeted us at the door with a huge cake decorated in team colors.
all.zh: 母親在門口迎接我們，端著一個以球隊顏色裝飾的大蛋糕。

all.en: The next day, news headlines praised our team's incredible comeback against all odds.
all.zh: 隔天的新聞頭條讚揚我們的球隊在劣勢中完成驚人逆轉。

all.en: I posted a photo of my ticket online, and old classmates instantly responded.
all.zh: 我把票根的照片發到網路上，老同學們立刻紛紛留言。

all.en: My father said he had not felt that proud and emotional in many years.
all.zh: 父親說他已經很多年沒有感受到那樣的驕傲與激動了。

all.en: From that day on, baseball became our special bond that nothing could replace.
all.zh: 從那天起，棒球成了我們之間獨特而無可取代的連結。

all.en: A month later, the team invited season ticket holders to a special meet and greet.
all.zh: 一個月後，球隊邀請了季票持有者參加一場特別的見面會。

all.en: My father and I waited in line for two hours to shake hands with the players.
all.zh: 我和父親排了兩個小時的隊，只為了和球員們握手。

all.en: The cleanup hitter remembered the championship moment and signed my poster personally.
all.zh: 那位第四棒打者還記得冠軍賽的瞬間，並親自為我的海報簽名。

all.en: He told us his home run was the proudest moment of his entire career.
all.zh: 他告訴我們，那支全壘打是他整個職業生涯中最自豪的時刻。

all.en: My father quietly wiped a tear from his eye while listening to the story.
all.zh: 父親在聆聽故事時，悄悄拭去眼角的一滴淚。

all.en: On the way home, we stopped by the stadium one more time to take photos.
all.zh: 回家路上，我們又繞到球場一次拍照留念。

all.en: My father decided to frame the signed poster and hang it in our living room.
all.zh: 父親決定把簽名海報裱框，掛在我們的客廳裡。

all.en: Now whenever guests visit, the conversation always begins with that legendary championship game.
all.zh: 現在每當客人來訪，話題總是從那場傳奇的冠軍賽開始。

all.en: My father promised to take me to the opening game of every season from now on.
all.zh: 父親承諾從今以後每年都帶我去看開幕戰。

all.en: Baseball was no longer just a sport but a tradition that belonged to us both.
all.zh: 棒球不再只是一項運動，而是屬於我們父子倆共同的傳統。

all.en: During spring training, my father and I drove three hours to watch practice games.
all.zh: 春訓期間，我和父親開了三小時的車去觀看練習賽。

all.en: The cleanup hitter spotted us in the front row and waved with a wide smile.
all.zh: 那位第四棒打者在前排看到我們，露出燦爛的微笑揮手致意。

all.en: He invited us into the dugout for a quick tour after the morning practice.
all.zh: 晨間練習結束後，他邀請我們進入休息區進行簡短的參觀。

all.en: My father was speechless as he stood on the field where champions had played.
all.zh: 父親站在冠軍曾征戰過的球場上，激動得說不出話來。

all.en: The coach overheard our story and gave me a baseball signed by the entire team.
all.zh: 教練聽到我們的故事，送了我一顆全隊簽名的棒球。

all.en: On the drive home, we listened to old broadcasts of legendary playoff games together.
all.zh: 回家的路上，我們一起聆聽傳奇季後賽的舊播報錄音。

all.en: My father said this trip had healed something deep inside his quiet heart.
all.zh: 父親說這趟旅行療癒了他內心深處某個沉默的角落。

all.en: That summer, we volunteered together at a youth baseball camp for underprivileged children.
all.zh: 那年夏天，我們一起在一個為弱勢兒童舉辦的青少年棒球營擔任志工。

all.en: My father coached pitching while I taught the basics of batting stance and timing.
all.zh: 父親教導投球，而我則教打擊站姿和揮棒時機的基礎。

all.en: Years later, the signed baseball still sits beside the framed poster in our living room.
all.zh: 多年後，那顆簽名棒球仍擺在我們客廳那張裱框海報的旁邊。

all.en: That autumn, the team announced a special charity exhibition game for local youth programs.
all.zh: 那年秋天，球隊宣布為當地青少年計畫舉辦一場特別的慈善表演賽。

all.en: My father bought front row tickets again, this time inviting my mother to join us.
all.zh: 父親再次買了前排門票，這次邀請母親一同前往。

all.en: She had never attended a live baseball game and was surprised by the festive atmosphere.
all.zh: 她從未親臨棒球賽，被現場熱鬧的氛圍深深震撼。

all.en: The cleanup hitter recognized our family and waved warmly from the practice batting cage.
all.zh: 那位第四棒打者認出了我們一家，從打擊練習籠裡溫暖地揮手致意。

all.en: My mother shyly waved back, holding a small homemade banner she had quietly prepared.
all.zh: 母親害羞地揮手回應，手裡舉著她默默準備的一面小小手作橫幅。

all.en: During the seventh inning, the stadium screen highlighted families enjoying the game together.
all.zh: 第七局時，球場大螢幕特別播出一家人共享比賽的溫馨畫面。

all.en: To our shock, our smiling faces appeared on the giant screen for everyone to see.
all.zh: 令我們震驚的是，我們的笑臉竟出現在巨型螢幕上供全場觀看。

all.en: My father blushed deeply while my mother laughed and waved with both her hands.
all.zh: 父親滿臉通紅，母親則笑著用雙手揮舞致意。

all.en: The team donated all ticket sales to the same youth baseball camp we had supported.
all.zh: 球隊將所有票房收入捐給我們曾支持的那個青少年棒球營。

all.en: On the train home, my mother said baseball had finally become her favorite family tradition.
all.zh: 回家的火車上，母親說棒球終於成了她最喜愛的家族傳統。

all.en: The next spring, our local youth baseball camp welcomed twenty new children with donated gloves and bats.
all.zh: 次年春天，我們當地的青年棒球營以捐贈的手套和球棒迎來了二十位新孩子。

all.en: My father volunteered as a quiet morning assistant coach, gently retrieving balls from the dewy outfield grass.
all.zh: 我父親擔任安靜的晨間助理教練，溫柔地從沾露的外野草地上撿球。

all.en: My mother brought thermoses of warm soy milk to share with shivering parents along the cold fence.
all.zh: 我母親帶著保溫瓶裝的熱豆漿，與寒冷圍欄旁顫抖的家長們分享。

all.en: One shy girl from Vietnam learned to swing her first solid hit during her third quiet practice.
all.zh: 一位來自越南的害羞女孩，在她第三次安靜的練習中，揮出了她紮實的第一支安打。

all.en: Her father wept silently behind the dugout, gripping his weathered work cap tightly with shaking hands.
all.zh: 她的父親在球員休息區後方默默落淚，顫抖的雙手緊握著磨損的工作帽。

all.en: By summer, she became one of our most reliable second basemen during our small weekend league games.
all.zh: 到了夏天，她成為我們小型週末聯盟賽中最可靠的二壘手之一。

all.en: The professional team invited her family to a free game in their reserved community appreciation box.
all.zh: 那支職業隊邀請她們全家到他們保留的社區感謝包廂，免費觀賞一場比賽。

all.en: She wore my old jersey, gently passed down with my mother's careful new embroidered name tag.
all.zh: 她穿著我的舊球衣，由母親細心地繡上新名牌後溫柔地傳承下來。

all.en: She cheered loudly when the team's young star ran straight to the dugout fence to greet her.
all.zh: 當那位年輕球星直接跑到休息區圍欄前跟她打招呼時，她大聲歡呼。

all.en: Baseball had quietly grown into many gloves passed between strangers who slowly became one tender shared family.
all.zh: 棒球，已悄悄成長為許多手套，在陌生人之間傳遞，慢慢凝聚成一個動人的共享家族。

all.en: By her second season, the Vietnamese girl led the camp's youngest squad as their quietly determined team captain.
all.zh: 到了她的第二個球季，那位越南女孩以安靜堅定的姿態，擔任了營隊最年幼小隊的隊長。

all.en: She taught new arrivals how to grip the bat, breathe slowly and trust their own steady arms.
all.zh: 她教新來的孩子如何握棒、慢慢呼吸，並信任自己穩定的雙臂。

all.en: My father designed simple printed instruction cards in three languages for the camp's growing international families.
all.zh: 父親為營隊日益增多的國際家庭設計了三種語言版本的簡易印刷教學卡片。

all.en: My mother organized a small potluck where every family brought one dish from their home country.
all.zh: 母親籌辦了一場小型百樂餐，邀請每個家庭從自己的家鄉帶來一道菜。

all.en: We ate spring rolls, dumplings, tamales and beef noodle soup beneath the warm stadium floodlights together.
all.zh: 我們在溫暖的球場照明燈下一起享用了春捲、餃子、玉米粽和牛肉麵。

all.en: The team's young star showed up unannounced and joined us for a humble plate of homemade food.
all.zh: 那位年輕球星突然不請自來，加入我們享用了一盤樸實的家常菜。

all.en: He told the children that championship rings were nice but family suppers tasted infinitely sweeter to him.
all.zh: 他告訴孩子們，冠軍戒指雖然美好，但家庭晚餐對他而言滋味無比甘甜。

all.en: By winter, three more camp graduates earned scholarships to play at competitive city high school baseball programs.
all.zh: 到了冬天，又有三位營隊畢業生獲得獎學金，進入競爭激烈的市區高中棒球計畫。

all.en: My father framed every camp photograph along a long wooden hallway he had quietly built himself.
all.zh: 父親將每張營隊照片裱框，沿著他親手安靜打造的長長木質走廊掛了一整排。

all.en: One championship ticket had truly grown into many hallways of photographs holding our quietly widening shared family.
all.zh: 一張冠軍賽門票，已真正成長為一整條條走廊的照片，承載著我們悄悄擴展的共享家族。

all.en: By the tenth summer, our small camp had quietly grown into an island-wide youth baseball mentorship network.
all.zh: 到了第十個夏天，我們的小營隊已悄悄成長為一個遍及全島的青年棒球師徒網絡。

all.en: Over forty volunteer coaches traveled monthly between rural towns, teaching gentle fundamentals to children without paid trainers.
all.zh: 超過四十位志工教練每月奔波於偏鄉小鎮之間，為沒有付費教練的孩子教授溫柔的基本動作。

all.en: My father, now nearly eighty, still attended every championship game in a wheelchair pushed gently by his grandson.
all.zh: 如今將近八十歲的父親，仍由孫子溫柔推著輪椅出席每一場冠軍賽。

all.en: He kept careful score on yellowed notebook pages, calling them his quiet record of three generations sharing one diamond.
all.zh: 他在泛黃的筆記本頁面上仔細記分，稱它們為三代人共享同一座菱形球場的安靜紀錄。

all.en: One former camp graduate became a national professional player and visited our humble field every winter to coach beginners.
all.zh: 一位過去的營隊畢業生成為國家職業球員，每年冬天回到我們樸實的球場指導初學者。

all.en: He brought his old jersey, hanging it inside our small clubhouse beside dozens of carefully framed camp photographs.
all.zh: 他帶來自己的舊球衣，掛在我們小小的會所內，與數十張仔細裱框的營隊照片並列。

all.en: Local families donated equipment, fresh fruit and homemade lunches throughout every long, warm summer training season together.
all.zh: 在地家庭在整個漫長溫暖的夏季訓練期間，捐贈了器材、新鮮水果與自製便當。

all.en: A documentary crew filmed our final championship game, with my father's gentle scorekeeping featured throughout the closing scenes.
all.zh: 一個紀錄片團隊拍攝了我們最後的冠軍賽，父親溫柔的記分動作貫穿了片尾。

all.en: Viewers across the country mailed handwritten letters thanking him for quietly tending baseball across so many patient decades.
all.zh: 全國各地的觀眾寄來手寫信，感謝他在數個耐心年代裡安靜地守護著棒球。

all.en: One championship ticket had truly grown into a wheelchair scoring three generations of softly diamond-loving hearts together.
all.zh: 一張冠軍賽門票，已真正成長為一輛輪椅，為三代共愛菱形球場的溫柔心靈一同記分。

all.en: I still keep my championship ticket stub inside the same wallet I used in high school over a decade ago.
all.zh: 我那張冠軍賽票根，到現在還夾在我十幾年前高中時用的同一個皮夾裡。

all.en: My father texts me every opening day at exactly seven in the morning to remind me to wear my cap.
all.zh: 每年開幕戰那天早上七點整，父親都會傳訊息提醒我戴上那頂冠軍帽。

all.en: Last weekend, my five-year-old nephew asked who was the man in the picture with grandpa at the stadium.
all.zh: 上週末，我五歲的姪子問起照片中和阿公一起在球場合照的那個人是誰。

all.en: I explained the home run story in simple words, and now he wants a real baseball glove for his birthday.
all.zh: 我用簡單的話跟他解釋那支全壘打的故事，現在他生日就想要一個真的棒球手套。

all.en: Our family group chat still erupts every time the team plays an extra-innings game on a weekday evening.
all.zh: 每次球隊在平日晚上打延長賽，我們家族群組就會立刻熱鬧起來。

all.en: Mom finally learned what an RBI means after five seasons, and she gloats about it at every dinner.
all.zh: 經過五個球季，媽終於搞懂什麼是打點，現在每次吃飯都拿出來炫耀。

all.en: I bought my father a soft cushion for his stadium seat because his back hurts now during long games.
all.zh: 我幫父親買了一塊軟坐墊放在球場座位上，因為他現在看長場比賽腰會痛。

all.en: Last week, my nephew swung his tiny plastic bat in the living room and shouted that he hit a home run.
all.zh: 上週，我姪子在客廳揮著他的小塑膠球棒，大喊他打出了一支全壘打。

all.en: We pulled the framed poster down briefly so he could touch the cleanup hitter's signature with his small fingers.
all.zh: 我們暫時把裱框海報拿下來，讓他用小手指摸一摸那位第四棒打者的簽名。

all.en: I realized that the championship game had never really ended for our family at all.
all.zh: 我這才明白，那場冠軍賽對我們家來說，從來就沒有真正結束過。
